join_order.notes: Build bitset[2000001] of qualifying p_partkeys (256 KB, fits in L3). Probe with l_partkey using direct array bit-test. O(1) per lineitem row.
Probe partsupp_pk_hash with composite key (int64: l_partkey<<32 | uint32(l_suppkey)). splitmix64 hash. Fetch ps_supplycost by row_id.
Probe orders_orderkey_hash with l_orderkey (knuth mult hash). Fetch o_orderdate by row_id. Extract year via Howard Hinnant civil_from_days.
Probe supplier_suppkey_hash with l_suppkey (knuth mult hash). Fetch s_nationkey by row_id.
Load all 25 nation rows at startup into string array indexed by n_nationkey (values 0-24). Direct array access, zero hash overhead.

data_structures.part_filter: bitset(2000001) — 256 KB direct-indexed by p_partkey value; set bit for each qualifying part after LIKE scan
data_structures.partsupp_join: pre_built_index(partsupp_pk_hash) — composite int64 key, splitmix64 hash, linear probe
data_structures.orders_join: pre_built_index(orders_orderkey_hash) — int32 key, knuth mult hash, linear probe
data_structures.supplier_join: pre_built_index(supplier_suppkey_hash) — int32 key, knuth mult hash, linear probe
data_structures.nation_lookup: flat_array[25] of n_name strings, indexed by n_nationkey — preloaded at startup
data_structures.aggregation: thread_local direct_array[25][8] of double — indexed by (nation_id 0..24, year_offset from base_year). Merge after parallel scan. ~175 groups total.

preload_steps.action: Read nation/n_nationkey.bin and nation/n_name varlen; populate string nation_names[25] indexed by n_nationkey value
mmap partsupp_pk_hash.bin, orders_orderkey_hash.bin, supplier_suppkey_hash.bin (read-only, shared across threads)
Single-threaded scan of part/p_name varlen (2M rows, fast); set bit in qualifying_parts bitset[2000001] for each row where memmem(data+start, len, 'green', 5) != nullptr

compute.year_extraction: Howard Hinnant civil_from_days: z+=719468; era=(z>=0?z:z-146096)/146097; doe=z-era*146097; yoe=(doe-doe/1460+doe/36524-doe/146096)/365; y=yoe+era*400; doy=doe-(365*yoe+yoe/4-yoe/100); mp=(5*doy+2)/153; if(mp>=10) y++; return y;

parallelism.parallel_phase: scan_lineitem_probe_join_aggregate — split lineitem rows into morsels of ~100K rows; each thread owns local agg array[25][8]
parallelism.notes: Part bitset read-only during parallel phase (no synchronization needed). Index files mmap'd read-only. Thread-local agg arrays avoid any atomic operations.

aggregation.thread_strategy: thread_local arrays, reduce/merge after parallel scan completes

output.notes: Trivially cheap sort of 175 rows after aggregation merge
